zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward Herbert於1544年6月出生在英國米德萊可亨登時，他的父親Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke43歲，母親Anne Parr29歲。他於大約1570年與Mary Stanley在英國賀特福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他在英國維爾爾笛卡爾 薩盧姆被列為minister of parlement。他去世於1594年3月23日在威爾士蒙格莫利夏爾波維斯 卡素享年49歲，埋葬在英國威爾斯波斯維爾申普爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edward Herbert于1544年6月出生在英国米德萊可亨登时，他的父亲Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke43岁，母亲Anne Parr29岁。他于大约1570年与Mary Stanley在英国賀特福爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他在英国維爾爾笛卡爾 薩盧姆被列为minister of parlement。他去世于1594年3月23日在威尔士蒙格莫利夏爾波維斯 卡素享年49岁，埋葬在英国威尔斯波斯維爾申普爾。

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Harbison
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name Herbert (the change from -er- to -ar- was a common one in Old French and Middle English). An intrusive n later gave Harbinson .

variants.name: Hebard
meanings: English (Surrey):
variant of Herbert , with assimilation of the first r. Compare Hebb .
variant of   Hibbert  .

variants.name: Harbert
meanings: German and English: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbers
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from   Herbert  . It is found mainly in Lower Saxony and across the Dutch border.

variants.name: Dickherber
meanings: Probably a nickname for a fat person by the name of Herbert , of which Herber is a short form, or, more likely, in the north from Die(c)kherber, a topographic and distinguishing name for a Herber living by the dike, see Dicke 2. Compare Dickmeyer .

variants.name: Hebert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .
German: variant of   Heber   1.

variants.name: Herber
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbertson
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name   Herbert  .

variants.name: Hebb
meanings: English (East Yorkshire and northeast Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Hebb, Ebb, pet forms of Herbert .

variants.name: Herb
meanings: South German: nickname for a harsh or rough person, from Middle High German herb ‘tart, harsh’.
German: from the personal name Herpo, a pet form of Herbert .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward Herbert was born in June 1544, in Hendon, Middlesex, England, his father, Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke, was 43 and his mother, Anne Parr, was 29. He married Mary Stanley about 1570, in Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. His occupation is listed as minister of parlement in Old Sarum, Wiltshire, England. He died on 23 March 1594, in Powis Castle, Montgomeryshire, Wales, at the age of 49, and was buried in Welshpool, Powys, Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edward Herbert est né en juin 1544, à Hendon, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Anne Parr, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Mary Stanley environ 1570, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est minister of parlement à Old Sarum, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 23 mars 1594, à Powis Castle, Montgomeryshire, Galles, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Welshpool, Powys, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edward Herbert im Juni 1544 in Hendon, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke 43 und seine Mutter Anne Parr 29. Er hat um 1570 in Hertfordshire, England Mary Stanley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 7 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als minister of parlement in Old Sarum, Wiltshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 23. März 1594 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Powis Castle, Montgomeryshire, Wales und wurde in Welshpool, Powys, Wales, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Mary Stanley circa 1570, nel Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 7 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come minister of parlement nel Old Sarum, Wiltshire, England. Morì il 23 marzo 1594, nel Powis Castle, Montgomeryshire, Galles, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Welshpool, Powys, Galles, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Herbertが1544年6月にイギリスミッドレセックスヘンドンで生まれた時，父Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembrokeは43歳，母Anne Parrは29歳であった。約1570年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアでMary Stanleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。イギリスウィルトシュアイアオルド サルムでの職業はminister of parlementと記録されている。彼は1594年3月23日にウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアポウィス カストルで49歳で死去し，イギリスウェールズポウイスウエルシュプオオルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Herbert(이)가 1544년 6월 영국 미들레세스 헨던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Parr(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1570년 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 Mary Stanley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 윌츠히르 드 사럼에서 minister of parlement이었다. 그는 1594년 3월 23일 웨일스 몽고머이실르 파위스 카스틀에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨일스 파우이스 웰쉬포에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Herbert nasceu em junho de 1544, em Hendon, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Anne Parr, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Stanley aproximadamente 1570, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como minister of parlement em Old Sarum, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 23 de março de 1594, em Powis Castle, Montgomeryshire, Gales, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Welshpool, Powys, Gales, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward Herbert родился июнь 1544 г. в местности хендон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Parr, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Stanley около 1570 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии minister of parlement в местности олд сарум, уилцхир, Англия. Он умер 23 марта 1594 г. в местности поуис кастл, монтгомиришир, Уэльс в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности велшпул, поуис, Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edward Herbert nació en junio de 1544, en Hendon, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Herbert 1st Earl of Pembroke, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Anne Parr, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Mary Stanley aproximadamente 1570, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era minister of parlement en Old Sarum, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 23 de marzo de 1594, en Powis Castle, Montgomeryshire, Gales, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Welshpool, Powys, Gales, Reino Unido.
